capability: UC-010 dispatch asset generation (SEAM A) — cartoon variant, live GPU proof
model: base Stable Diffusion XL 1.0, OpenVINO INT8 (nncf weight-only) + DD-vector flat-vector LoRA (runtime AdapterConfig, alpha 0.8, NOT fused)
methodology: Two sequential generates (seed 42 then 7) via a direct image_gen.generate_text2image on the real signed-manifest-verified model. First = load+generate, second = generate-only (pipeline resident). RAM sampled at 5 Hz for the peak. Standalone (no resident 14B).
prompt_example: vector illustration of a friendly cartoon elephant waving hello, flat design, bold outlines, solid color background
not_measured: 14B co-residence — this standalone generate runs WITHOUT the resident 14B. The 14B+SDXL co-resident peak (~26.0 GB, 5.3 GB headroom) is the ADR-033 Memory Phase-0 record (docs/performance/image_gen_phase0_2026-06-16.json); SEAM A runs in that envelope (base 1024^2 keeps the 14B). VLM/voice co-residence not measured. 30B-coder co-residence with the image model is FORBIDDEN by design (32.5 GB breach) — hence SEAM A generates pre-swap.
notes: First community-grade numbers for the dispatch asset generator. 30 steps at ~2 s/step is the quality-tuned EULER_ANCESTRAL cost; a draft-quality dispatch asset can lower steps (config knob). The generated cartoon elephant is the SEAM-A 'after' that replaces testproject6's hand-drawn inline SVG.
